name: MICHAEL LEE INDOOR GOLF CENTER
address: Volk Gymnasium, 1365 Maple St, Golden, CO 80401
about: The Michael Lee Indoor Golf Center is an NCAA Division II caliber indoor golf facility located on the ground floor of Volk Gymnasium. Completed in 2015 and dedicated in 2022, it serves as the year-round practice home for the Colorado School of Mines Orediggers golf teams. The facility features a GolfTec g-SWING hitting bay equipped with a launch monitor and video review capabilities, complemented by a full-size putting and chipping green. It also includes team lockers, a study area, and a lounge. An adjacent partner course, Bear Creek Golf Club in Lakewood, serves as the team's home course.
why_golfers_like_it: Premium GolfTec g-SWING technology with launch monitor and video review for detailed swing analysis
Full-size putting and chipping green for comprehensive short game practice
Dedicated team facilities including lockers and a study/lounge area
Associated with a championship-quality 18-hole course designed by Arnold Palmer
Facility is dedicated to the memory of a celebrated all-American golfer, emphasizing a serious golfing legacy

food_and_drink.notes: No on-site food service is mentioned
No on-site alcohol service is mentioned

simulator_hardware.notes: Launch monitor and video review system

faq.answer: Players are expected to bring their own clubs to use the facility's bays and practice areas.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: No specific information is provided regarding outside food or drink policies.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: As an indoor practice facility focused on hitting and short game, 18-hole simulation times are not specified.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: The facility is described as a dedicated indoor practice home for a collegiate golf team, suggesting it is focused on serious practice rather than a family-friendly atmosphere.
